name: Patently
one_liner: AI IP-clearance research for founders & product teams — "Ship without blindsiding yourself on IP." Research chat, clearance memos, and watchlist digests over US case law, patents, and copyright registrations.

stack.backend: Neon Postgres (HTTP serverless) + pgvector 1024-dim + HNSW index
stack.host: Vercel (hobby plan, auto-deploy from GitHub main)

ai.models: Claude Sonnet 4.6 (chat + clearance synthesis), Haiku 4.5 (fact extraction, digest summarise), Opus 4.7 (optional)

stack.auth: Clerk (test keys — swap to live before paid users)
stack.email: Resend (digest emails; sender domain not yet verified)

demo_pattern: User signs in → asks a copyright or patent question in chat → every citation links to a real case or filing → or pastes a product description into clearance → gets a structured memo with risk level, patent overlap, fair-use analysis, and next steps in 60-120s.
notes: Hamzaish-built proof product. Renamed IP Radar → Patently; LIVE in private beta at https://patently.legal (verified 2026-06-07, tagline "Ship without blindsiding yourself on IP."). Nine-step clearance DAG (extract_facts → parallel gather → claim_similarity + fair_use → synthesize → validate with one auto-correct retry). Citation contract validator rejects unknown IDs and bare Bluebook citations. Hit USPTO ID.me/SSN wall mid-build as a non-US user — pivoted to Google Patents BigQuery in same session (better architecture, no ID.me dependency, effectively free at prototype scale). GCP service-account key stored base64-encoded in Vercel envs after Vercel CLI multi-line stdin truncation bug. Personal-tier daily limits: 50 chat messages + 50 clearance memos per 24h rolling window. All-in cost tracking per clearance run (LLM + embeddings + BigQuery via AsyncLocalStorage). Admin dashboard at /admin (email-gated). Pending: credential rotation (all burned in old chat transcripts), Clerk webhook for digest email delivery, pnpm db:migrate for migration 0001 (cost columns). Built in ~6 weeks of sessions 2026-04-21 to 2026-06-02. Domain picked (was open): patently.legal.

validation.state: 5 beta users complete a real chat+clearance flow and give feedback (source: /admin usage + notes)

launch.state: P0s cleared (credentials rotated, privacy/terms live, cost caps) + 10 activated beta users (source: /admin)

traction.state: 3 paying customers OR >=40% of beta users return in week 2 (source: Stripe + admin usage)

pmf.state: Sean Ellis >=40% AND retention curve flattens (source: survey + usage)
